claim: O3 is expensive at $40 per million output tokens but represents state-of-the-art performance with an 8% improvement over Gemini 2.5 Pro on the Aider Polyglot Coding Leaderboard
actionable: For high-stakes coding tasks, reserve O3 for difficult problems; use GPT-4.1 or Gemini 2.5 Pro as default workhorse models to reduce costs while maintaining strong performance
source_title: Making o3 My CEO. Codex RIPS OFF Claude Code?! SOTA LLM Playbook

claim: Combining O3 in high mode with GPT-4.1 in architect mode achieves state-of-the-art performance while reducing costs compared to O3 alone
actionable: Use multi-model patterns where O3 plans (high reasoning mode) and GPT-4.1 executes; this maximizes performance while controlling cost
source_title: Making o3 My CEO. Codex RIPS OFF Claude Code?! SOTA LLM Playbook

claim: O3 makes 600+ tool calls for single difficult tasks, demonstrating that modern models are systems with extensive tool-calling capabilities, not just text generators
actionable: Design tool-calling interfaces for models that expect high-volume sequential calls; build robust error handling and short feedback loops for models that call tools 100+ times per task
source_title: Making o3 My CEO. Codex RIPS OFF Claude Code?! SOTA LLM Playbook

claim: Modern AI models are better at code generation than humans (programmer-level), but intelligence alone isn't sufficient—tool-crafting and agent design are now the limiting factors for engineering work
actionable: When building AI-powered engineering systems, focus on tool design and agent workflows rather than model selection; the models handle coding, you handle integration
source_title: Making o3 My CEO. Codex RIPS OFF Claude Code?! SOTA LLM Playbook

claim: Claude Code is significantly better than OpenAI's Codex; Codex lacks cost tracking, unnecessary system validation, and cleaner abstractions compared to Claude Code and other tools like Aider
actionable: For production AI coding, prioritize Claude Code or Aider over Codex; verify that your chosen tool provides cost visibility and doesn't waste tokens on redundant validation
source_title: Making o3 My CEO. Codex RIPS OFF Claude Code?! SOTA LLM Playbook

claim: Multi-model prompting pattern where you get opinions from multiple specialized models and have one CEO model synthesize them enables complex decision-making with consensus
actionable: For high-stakes decisions, prompt multiple models with different personas (expert, skeptic, optimist), collect their responses, and use a CEO model to synthesize and make final recommendation
source_title: Making o3 My CEO. Codex RIPS OFF Claude Code?! SOTA LLM Playbook

claim: Context window sizes (200k vs 1M tokens) limit what agents can do in single passes; larger context windows enable more ambitious single-turn tasks without context management overhead
actionable: When selecting models for agentic coding, prioritize larger context windows (500k+) to reduce need for sequential context management and file chunking
source_title: Making o3 My CEO. Codex RIPS OFF Claude Code?! SOTA LLM Playbook

claim: Context priming (providing file structure and README context upfront) is critical for AI coding tools to avoid wasting tokens figuring out codebase structure
actionable: Always provide directory structure, file organization, and high-level architecture in initial prompt to AI coding tools; this reduces wasted tokens and improves coding decisions
source_title: Making o3 My CEO. Codex RIPS OFF Claude Code?! SOTA LLM Playbook

claim: The Aider Polyglot Coding Leaderboard is the most reliable benchmark for AI coding tools because it's executable and verifiable by users, unlike opaque benchmarks
actionable: Prioritize benchmarks you can run yourself and verify directly; avoid benchmarks that require trusting a provider's results without reproducibility
source_title: Making o3 My CEO. Codex RIPS OFF Claude Code?! SOTA LLM Playbook
